author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

Shipped storage + CLI for the four memory verbs plus
`jjf show --include-memories`. Key shape decisions:

- Memory ops are independent of the per-issue `Op` enum.
  `set-memory` / `unset-memory` trailers omit `Jjf-Issue:`,
  so the existing per-issue trailer parser drops them
  silently — no pollution of any issue's op chain
  (asserted via a dedicated test). Avoided a noisy refactor
  of `Op::issue_id()` to return `Option<&IssueId>`.
- Reused the existing slug validator for memory keys
  (kebab-case, 3-48 chars). Same shape, same code path.
- Memory storage methods bypass the issue-mutate machinery
  via a dedicated `commit_memory_change` 4-CLI dance
  helper. They share the bookmark and the dance shape, but
  not the message builder or the file-family writers.
- Spec bumped to v2.2 (additive); v2.1 readers tolerate
  the new ops via the existing unknown-op-type rule.

Test coverage: 12 storage-crate tests (set/unset/upsert/list/read,
trailer shape, no-issue-pollution invariant) + 19 CLI integration
tests (all four verbs + `--include-memories`, JSON envelopes,
error paths). 307 workspace tests green; smoke-tested
end-to-end in a sibling jj repo.

## Recommendation

Close. Acceptance bullets all met:
- remember/recall round-trip (auto-slug + explicit `--key`).
- Upsert in place (`Updated [k]`).
- Forget removes; subsequent recall exits 1.
- `memories` lists; substring filter searches key OR value.
- `push`/`pull` carry the file family (bookmark transport,
  no special handling needed in the common case).
- `show roadmap --include-memories` appends the block.
- Spec docs updated (storage-format §10, cli-json memory
  verbs section).

## Confidence

High on the storage shape and the verb surface. Medium on
merge semantics under divergent edits to the same memory
key — that's deferred to a follow-up (rely on jj's textual
auto-merge for v1; users can pin the winner with a fresh
`jjf remember`). Per the ticket "Out of scope" section,
op-space memory resolution is explicitly a separate
ticket.

## Open follow-ups

- Op-space resolver for memories (deferred): fold
  `set-memory` / `unset-memory` ops by LWW in
  `merge_ops.rs`. Only matters when two clones edit the
  same key concurrently; uncommon for memory traffic.
- `jjf prime` verb that bundles roadmap + ready + memories
  in one call (ticket called it out as a follow-up).
- Memory history surfacer ("when was this memory set, by
  whom"). Op trailers carry it; surfacing is a follow-up.
- Memory categories / TTL / per-issue scoping — explicitly
  deferred by the ticket.
